title: Unbounded recursive file scan can crash terminal under PS7
description: A Get-ChildItem -Recurse with no excludes against a large workspace (.git, node_modules, .venv, logs, temp) followed by Get-Content -Raw on every match can silently consume gigabytes of memory and lose the terminal connection. Observed: Invoke-SecretScan against C:\PowerShellGUI root forced a 'Restarting the terminal because the connection to the shell process was lost' event. PowerShell never raises an OOM in this path; the host process simply exits.
fix_strategy: Always provide an ExcludeDir allowlist (.git, node_modules, .venv, logs, temp, checkpoints) before recursing.
Cap MaxFileSize (e.g. skip files > 5MB) before reading -Raw.
For workspace-wide tools, prefer streaming Select-String over Get-Content -Raw + regex.
